name: Altar Knights
summary: The Altar Knights of Caritas Catholic Chaplaincy are a dedicated fraternity of young men who serve at the foot of the Cross during liturgical celebrations. Combining medieval knightly values of chivalry, discipline, and reverence with deep Eucharistic devotion, the association is a pillar of liturgical excellence within the university community.
history: Established during the foundational years of Caritas University, the Altar Knights grew from a small group of volunteer servers into a structured association. Over the decades, they have maintained a tradition of rigorous training and spiritual formation, ensuring that every Holy Mass is celebrated with the highest degree of dignity and order.
about: The Altar Knights are tasked with assisting the Bishop, Priest, and Deacon in the sanctuary. Their roles range from thurifers (handling incense) and crucifers (carrying the processional cross) to acolytes and candle bearers. Beyond their visible duties, they are a community focused on 'spiritual combat'—developing a strong prayer life and moral character through the intercession of their patron, Saint Tarcisius, who died protecting the Blessed Sacrament.
why_join: Joining the Altar Knights offers a unique opportunity to grow in discipline, liturgical knowledge, and brotherhood. Members gain a front-row seat to the mysteries of the Faith and develop a profound reverence for the Eucharist that lasts a lifetime.
fun_facts: The association's hierarchy is modeled after historical orders of knighthood, complete with initiation rites and ranks that emphasize service over prestige.
meeting_time: Tuesdays and Saturdays at 7:00 PM (Sanctuary Training and Prayer)

name: Altar Decorators
summary: The Altar Decorators are the silent architects of beauty in the house of God. Responsible for the arrangement of flowers, the selection of liturgical linens, and the seasonal transformation of the sanctuary, they ensure that the physical environment of the Chaplaincy inspires awe and contemplation.
history: Born from the need to maintain the dignity of the sacred space as the university grew, the decorators began as a group of selfless students who dedicated their weekends to beautifying the chapel. They have since become an essential liturgical body, pivotal during major celebrations like Easter and Christmas.
about: Sanctuary art is a form of silent preaching. The decorators follow the liturgical calendar strictly, using colors and symbols that reflect the theological themes of each season. Their work is a hidden sacrifice, often performed in the quiet hours before dawn or late at night.
why_join: If you have an eye for beauty and a heart that finds God in aesthetics, this association allows you to offer your talents directly to the Lord. It is a peaceful, creative form of service that brings joy to the whole community.
fun_facts: The association often uses locally sourced flowers and incorporates symbolic arrangements that represent university departments during special academic masses.
meeting_time: Tuesdays at 7:00 PM and Saturdays at 11:00 AM (Sanctuary Preparation)

name: Caritas Central Choir
summary: As the primary musical voice of the Chaplaincy, the Caritas Central Choir lifts the hearts of the university community to God through the power of sacred music. Specializing in a wide range of styles—from ancient Gregorian Chant and polyphony to contemporary liturgical hymns—the choir serves as an essential bridge between the congregation and the Divine during the Mass.
history: The choir has been the heartbeat of the Chaplaincy since its inception. It has evolved into a diverse group of gifted students and faculty members who commit themselves to the daunting task of preparing music for weekly liturgies, solemn feasts, and university-wide celebrations. Their history is one of resilience, often practicing late into the night to achieve harmonic perfection for the glory of God.
about: Music in the liturgy is not entertainment; it is prayer twice said. The Central Choir is dedicated to this principle. Led by the Director of Music, members undergo vocal training and liturgical education. They are particularly known for their 'Christmas Nine Lessons and Carols' and their solemn renditions of Latin hymns during the Easter Triduum.
why_join: If you have a gift for music and a heart for worship, the choir is your home. You will not only improve your vocal skills but also find a close-knit family of believers who support each other in their academic and spiritual journeys.
fun_facts: The choir is famous for its legendary 6-hour practice sessions during the week of the Feast of St. Cecilia!
meeting_time: Tuesdays and Thursdays at 7:00 PM; Saturdays at 07:00 PM

name: Church Wardens
summary: The Church Wardens are the guardians of order, hospitality, and reverence within the Chaplaincy. Serving as the face of the Church to all who enter, they ensure that the liturgy proceeds smoothly and that every worshipper feels welcome and focused on the divine mysteries.
history: As the congregation at Caritas University grew, the need for an organized body to manage seating, collections, and security became critical. The Wardens were established to bridge the gap between administrative order and spiritual hospitality.
about: A Warden's duty is multifaceted: they manage the flow of the congregation, assist the elderly and visitors, collect the offerings of the faithful, and maintain the 'Great Silence' required in the presence of the Blessed Sacrament. They are trained in hospitality and basic emergency response.
why_join: Become an pillar of the community and learn the art of Christ-like hospitality. Serving as a Warden builds leadership, situational awareness, and a deep sense of responsibility toward the house of God.
fun_facts: Wardens are often referred to as the 'Custodians of the Peace' and are the first to arrive and last to leave the sanctuary.

name: Board of Lectors
summary: Lectors are the mouthpieces of God within the liturgy. The Board of Lectors at Caritas is a prestigious body of students and staff trained to proclaim the living Word of God with clarity, authority, and spiritual depth so that the Scriptures may resonate in the hearts of the faithful.
history: The Board was formed to ensure that the proclamation of the Word was given the importance it deserves. Recognizing that 'ignorance of Scripture is ignorance of Christ,' the chaplaincy established a rigorous vetting and training process for all who wish to read at the ambo.
about: A Lector's duty goes beyond reading; it involves a deep study of the texts. Members gather regularly for Bible study and workshops on public speaking, phonetics, and liturgical flow. They ensure that every reading is not just heard, but understood as a personal message from God to the congregation.
why_join: Lectors grow in their love for the Bible and develop high-level communication skills. It is a profound privilege to speak the words of God to His people and to be instrumental in their spiritual nourishment.
fun_facts: Our patron, St. Jerome, spent 30 years in a cave translating the Bible from Hebrew and Greek into Latin—the Vulgate!
meeting_time: Saturdays at 7:00 PM (Bible Study and Rehearsal)

name: Mary Queen of All Hearts
summary: Guided by the 'Total Consecration' spirituality of Saint Louis de Montfort, this society seeks to lead every heart to Jesus through Mary. It is a school of holiness that emphasizes the 'Slave of Love' relationship with our Blessed Mother.
history: The society was introduced to Caritas to provide a deeper, more mystical path of Marian devotion for students. It has become a lighthouse of spiritual stability, drawing members into a life-long commitment to Marian lived reality.
about: Members undergo a 33-day preparation for Total Consecration, a pivotal event in their spiritual lives. They commit to doing everything 'with Mary, in Mary, through Mary, and for Mary' so as to do it more perfectly for Jesus. They lead the university in the 'True Devotion' seminars.
why_join: Discover the 'shortest, easiest, and surest' path to Jesus. If you feel called to a deep, transformative relationship with the Mother of God, this society provides the theological and spiritual framework to achieve it.
fun_facts: Members often wear a small, symbolic iron chain on their wrist or waist to signify their 'happy slavery' to Jesus and Mary.

name: Jesus the Saviour Society
summary: This is the 'Home Society' of Caritas University, directly embodying the foundational charism of Very Rev. Fr. Prof. Emmanuel Matthew Paul Edeh. The society focuses on the mission of Jesus the Saviour: bringing salvation, dignity, and peace to all humanity through selfless service and prayer.
history: Directly linked to the spiritual vision that gave birth to Caritas and Madonna Universities, the Jesus the Saviour Society ensures that the founder's charism remains the core of every student's experience. It acts as the primary custodian of the university's Catholic identity.
about: The society's spirituality is centered on the person of Jesus the Saviour. Members commit to lives of 'practical charity'—helping those in need, resolving conflicts within the university, and leading the community in specific saviourite devotions. They are the guardians of the founder's philosophy of education for human dignity.
why_join: To be a member of this society is to be at the heart of Caritas. It is a path to understanding the deeper purpose of your education and developing a leadership style rooted in Christ-like service.
fun_facts: The society is part of a larger international movement of Saviourites founded in Nigeria and spreading globally.
meeting_time: First Sundays after the 8:00 AM Mass and Tuesdays by 5:00 PM

name: Saint Anthony of Padua Society
summary: Known as the 'Hammer of Heretics' and the 'Wonder Worker,' St. Anthony of Padua guides this vibrant society in a dual mission of intense intercessory prayer and hands-on charity. The society is a place of miracles, deep faith, and commitment to the poor.
history: The devotion to St. Anthony has deep roots in the Nigerian Catholic experience. At Caritas, the society was formed by students who had experienced the Saint's powerful intercession in their personal lives and wanted to share that light with the wider community.
about: The society is famous for its 'Thirteen Tuesdays' devotion, which draws large numbers of seekers. They are also known for 'St. Anthony's Bread'—a program where they raise funds and items to help indigent students and local orphanages, following the example of their patron's love for the poor.
why_join: If you are looking for a society with powerful prayer lives and a tangible impact on the lives of others through charity, St. Anthony's is for you. Learn the power of intercession and the joy of giving.
fun_facts: St. Anthony is the only saint known to have been visited by the Child Jesus while in prayer, which is why he is always pictured with Him.

name: Mother of Perpetual Help Society
summary: This society is a refuge for students seeking maternal comfort and divine assistance in the midst of academic and personal storms. Devoted to the miraculous Redemptorist icon of Mary, members find in her a 'Perpetual Help' that never fails.
history: The society began as a small prayer group in the university hostels, where students gathered to share their exam anxieties with Mary. It has since grown into a major society known for its colorful processions and deeply moving novena sessions.
about: The core of the society is the Perpetual Help Novena. Members study the rich symbolism of the icon—the falling sandal of Jesus, the instruments of the Passion, and Mary's compassionate eyes. They act as 'ambassadors of hope,' encouraging others to never despair regardless of the difficulty.
why_join: University life is stressful. Joining this society ensures you have a motherly presence to guide you through every trial. You will learn the value of persistent, trusting prayer.
fun_facts: The original icon (kept in Rome) was stolen from Crete and survived a shipwreck before being returned to public veneration!

name: Sacred Hearts Society
summary: Focused on the infinite love of Jesus Christ as symbolized by His Sacred Heart, this society is a community of reparation and adoration. Inspired by the revelations to St. Margaret Mary Alacoque, members seek to console the Heart of Jesus.
history: The devotion was firmly established at the university to anchor the hearts of students in the unconditional love of God. It has grown into a powerful devotional group known for its intense First Friday vigils.
about: The society emphasizes the 'Twelve Promises' made by Jesus to those who honor His Sacred Heart. Members practice the Enthronement of the Sacred Heart and are dedicated to making reparation for the sins of the world, especially those committed against the Eucharist.
why_join: Find rest for your soul in the Heart that has loved men so much. This society offers a path of emotional healing and spiritual depth through the contemplation of Christ's human and divine love.
fun_facts: The image of the Sacred Heart was promoted significantly by the Jesuit order following the 17th-century visions in Paray-le-Monial, France.
meeting_time: Thursdays at 5:00 PM; First Friday Vigils

name: Confraternity of the Most Holy Rosary (Rosarian Family)
summary: The Rosarian Family is a vibrant community of students and staff dedicated to the daily recitation and promotion of the Holy Rosary. It is a 'school of prayer' where members contemplate the lives of Jesus and Mary through the lens of the mysteries.
history: What began as a group of students praying under a tree has transformed into one of the Chaplaincy's largest devotional groups. The Confraternity is part of a global movement authorized by the Dominican Order to spread the Rosary.
about: Members commit to praying a full Rosary regularlly and participating in group 'Block Rosaries' within the university hostels. They are known for their spectacular Rosary processions and their knowledge of the 'Fifteen Promises' given by Mary to St. Dominic.
why_join: The Rosary is the 'weapon' of our times. By joining, you enter a family that will pray for you and with you, helping you stay spiritually grounded in the midst of your studies.
fun_facts: The Rosarian Family in Caritas is famous for their 'Rosary at Midnight' sessions during exam periods!
meeting_time: Daily at 6:00 PM; Sundays at 5:30 PM

name: Legion of Mary
summary: The Legion of Mary is 'Mary's Army'—the largest lay apostolic organization in the Catholic Church. At Caritas, it operates as a structured, disciplined body of students engaged in active evangelization, work of mercy, and spiritual formation.
history: Part of a global movement founded by Frank Duff in Dublin in 1921, the Legion has been active at Caritas for years. Its structure—of Praesidiums, Curias, and Senatus—provides a sense of belonging to a vast spiritual military focused on winning souls for Christ through Mary.
about: Legionaries meet weekly for prayers focused on the 'Catena Legionis.' They pair up to perform 'work'—visiting sick students, encouraging the fallen away to return to the sacraments, and leading hostel rosaries. It is a life of active, humble service under the banner of our Lady.
why_join: If you want a disciplined path to holiness that combines prayer with action, the Legion is unmatched. You will develop courage, leadership, and a profound sense of mission.
fun_facts: The Legion's handbook is exactly the same in every country in the world, translated into hundreds of languages!

name: Infant Jesus Society
summary: Commemorating the childhood of our Lord, the Infant Jesus Society promotes a spirituality of simplicity, trust, and total dependence on Divine Providence. It reminds the university community to approach God with the heart of a child.
history: Introduced to the chaplaincy to help students manage the stresses of academic competition, the society emphasizes that God cares for our smallest needs. It is particularly popular for its novenas during the Christmas season.
about: The devotion centers on the miraculous 'Infant Jesus of Prague' statue. Members focus on 'Spiritual Childhood' as taught by St. Therese of Lisieux. They are involved in child welfare works and often organize events for staff children and local daycares.
why_join: In a world that demands we be self-sufficient and hardened, this society offers the freedom of being a child of God. Learn to trust that 'The more you honor Me, the more I will bless you.'
fun_facts: The statue of the Infant Jesus of Prague is historically dressed in different colored robes that change according to the liturgical season!

name: Two Hearts Society
summary: Focusing on the mystical and unbreakable union between the Sacred Heart of Jesus and the Immaculate Heart of Mary, this society is a community of intense spiritual life, prayer, and reparation.
history: A relatively recent addition to the Chaplaincy's spiritual tapestry, the Two Hearts Society was founded by students who felt called to a more contemplative and reparative form of devotion.
about: The society emphasizes that Mary's heart is the entryway to the Heart of Jesus. They practice regular Eucharistic Adoration and are committed to living a life of purity and sacrifice for the conversion of sinners and the peace of the world.
why_join: If you seek a deep, united devotion that honors both our Lord and His Blessed Mother, this society provides the perfect environment. Grow in the virtues of purity, humility, and sacrificial love.
fun_facts: The 'Two Hearts' are often depicted together because spiritually, whatever Mary felt, Jesus felt too, and vice versa.

name: Charismatic Renewal
summary: The Catholic Charismatic Renewal is a 'fire' within the Chaplaincy, bringing a dynamic experience of the Holy Spirit, spontaneous praise, and the exercise of 'charisms' or spiritual gifts for the building up of the Church.
history: Stemming from the worldwide movement that began at Duquesne University in 1967, the Renewal at Caritas has brought a new energy of worship and evangelization to the university since its earliest days.
about: The 'Life in the Spirit' seminars are the society's hallmark, leading members to the 'Baptism in the Holy Spirit.' Their prayer meetings are characterized by lively music, speaking in tongues, healing prayers, and prophetic words.
why_join: Experience the power of Pentecost today! If you are looking for a vibrant, expressive, and spirit-filled way to encounter God, the Renewal will ignite your faith like never before.
fun_facts: The movement is so influential that it has several 'Covenants' or 'Communities' globally that live a common life of praise.

name: Precious Blood of Jesus Society
summary: Dedicated to the adoration of the Blood of Christ shed for our redemption, this society is a contemplative and intercessory body that anchors the Chaplaincy in the price of our salvation.
history: Rooted in the spirituality of St. Gaspar, the 'Apostle of the Precious Blood,' the society was established at Caritas to foster a deeper appreciation for the redeeming power of the Cross.
about: Devotion centers on the 'Seven Effusions' of the Precious Blood—from the Circumcision to the Piercing of the Side. Members are committed to making reparation for the sins that caused Christ to bleed and to praying for the conversion of the most hardened sinners.
why_join: Meditate on the 'Price of our Salvation.' This society offers a path of deep spiritual maturity and a profound encounter with the sacrificial love of Jesus. It is a community of silence, power, and redemption.
fun_facts: St. Gaspar, the patron, was so influential that he cleared an entire town of dangerous bandits simply through his preaching on the Precious Blood!

name: Divine Mercy Society
summary: Dedicated to spreading the message of Jesus' fathomless mercy, this society is the spiritual oasis of the Chaplaincy. Members gather daily at the 3 o'clock 'Hour of Great Mercy' to pray for the whole world, emphasizing that no sin is greater than God's love.
history: Following the global spread of the devotion initiated by St. Faustina and promoted by St. John Paul II, the Divine Mercy society at Caritas was established to bring a message of hope and healing to the student body.
about: The society is most visible at 3:00 PM every day when they lead the 'Chaplet of Divine Mercy.' They also focus on the 'ABC' of mercy: Ask for His Mercy, Be Merciful to others, and Completely trust in Jesus. They run mercy-focused outreach programs during the Lenten season.
why_join: Learn to live a life free of shame and full of trust. Experience the profound peace that comes from the ocean of God's mercy and learn how to be a channel of that mercy to your peers.
fun_facts: Jesus requested that the image of Divine Mercy be painted exactly as seen in vision in 1931.
